epigraph.text: No one in this world has ever lost money by underestimating the intelligence of the great masses of the plain people.

follyOfTheDay.headline: University Bans Word 'Coconut' to Protect Student Sensibilities
follyOfTheDay.summary: A prestigious Northeastern institution has embarked upon the noblest of crusades: the complete eradication of a fruit from civilized discourse. Administrators have decreed that the word 'coconut' shall no longer grace the menus of the dining hall, nor shall it pollute the ears of impressionable undergraduates during lecture hours. The fruit itself—that innocent orb of fiber and water—has been deemed insufficiently inclusive for campus consumption, its very nomenclature a potential vector for offense. Faculty have been instructed to refer to coconut-containing products by euphemism or silence. This ambitious experiment in linguistic purification marks yet another triumph of institutional resolve over the ancient demons of everyday language. One wonders what the Greeks, Romans, and medieval scholastics would make of a civilization so thoroughly consumed with protecting its youth from the depredations of a nut.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: Higher education achieves new heights of absurdity whilst inflation renders degrees worthless and universities collapse under bloated administrative staffs. The coconut ban is but the latest monument to institutional stupidity.
